nativeRange.habitat: Rocky subtidal reef environments; grows on hard substrate in the photic zone

imageCandidates.notes: Primary product photo from Mosaic Macros listing; sole hobby trade source for this species.

summary: Orange Galaxy is a rare ornamental red macroalgae (Halymenia stipitata) with a small, rounded, bushy form. Under actinic lighting, its fleshy blades display vivid orange fluorescent dots that give the algae a star-field appearance — the source of its trade name. It is seldom seen in the aquarium hobby.
careNotes:
Orange Galaxy belongs to the family Halymeniaceae, order Halymeniales — the same family as the better-known Dragon's Breath (H. durvillei), Leafy Ogo (H. maculata), and Dragon's Tongue (H. dilatata). The older scientific name Augophyllum wysorii is still used in some trade contexts, but Halymenia stipitata is the currently accepted name. It is among the rarest Halymenia species available in the aquarium hobby. The thallus forms a small, round bushy shape with fleshy blades and tops out at roughly 2–3 inches (approximately 5–8 cm) in diameter, making it notably smaller than sibling species. Under actinic lighting, vivid orange fluorescent dots appear across the blades, creating a star-field or galaxy effect — the origin of the trade name. It is also occasionally sold under the trade name Atomic Broccoli Algae.

Detailed husbandry data for this species is limited, so care parameters are drawn from sibling Halymenia species. A temperature of approximately 24–28°C, pH 7.8–8.4, salinity SG 1.023–1.025, and alkalinity of 8–12 dKH are generally advisable based on conditions documented for H. durvillei and H. dilatata. Medium-intensity lighting is generally recommended; high-intensity lighting has been associated with browning in related Halymenia species, and moderate blue-spectrum light tends to be preferred to bring out the fluorescent coloration. Growth rate is typically slow to medium. Propagation in the aquarium is primarily by fragmentation.

Orange Galaxy is generally considered reef-safe with corals and peaceful invertebrates, but, like other Halymenia species, it is likely palatable to herbivorous fish. Tangs, rabbitfish, and sea urchins should be kept away from this algae. Iron and iodine supplementation may support coloration, consistent with general guidance for red macroalgae.

ATTACHMENT WARNING: as a red alga, Orange Galaxy will MELT at the base if buried in sand. It must be attached to rock using superglue, zip-tie, fishing line, or by wedging it securely into a crevice. Sand-bed placement is suitable only for green algae genera such as Caulerpa, Halimeda, Penicillus, Rhipocephalus, and Udotea, as well as some calcified Dasycladales.

primary.name: Mosaic Macros
primary.notes: Sole hobby trade source for this species. Confirmed scientific name Halymenia stipitata (synonym Augophyllum wysorii); described as small round bushy thallus with orange fluorescent dots under actinic, ~2–3 inches max diameter, seldom seen in hobby, highly sensitive to shipping.

additional.name: Dragon's Breath sibling reference (H. durvillei)
additional.notes: Used for family-level water parameter extrapolation (Halymeniaceae). H. durvillei: temp 25.6–28.3°C, pH 7.8–8.4, SG 1.023–1.025, kH 8–12.

additional.name: Dragon's Tongue sibling reference (H. dilatata)
additional.notes: Used for family-level temperature cross-check. H. dilatata: temp 22–27°C. Averaged with H. durvillei 25.6–28.3°C to derive 24–28°C for H. stipitata.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros general macroalgae care guide
additional.notes: Slice-wide secondary source — provides authoritative Mosaic-wide parameter targets (temp 22-26°C, pH 8.1-8.4, Ca 400-450 ppm, Mg 1250-1350 ppm, NO3 5-20 ppm, PO4 0.02-0.1 ppm) and the 4-method propagation standard. Added retroactively 2026-06-08.
